summary: Crisp matrix recording captures an explosive show highlighted by blistering Loser, Looks Like Rain, and a smoking China‑Cat/I Know You Rider.
review: The upgraded 16/24‑bit matrix source is exceptionally clear, giving the band’s performance full presence. Jerry’s “machine‑gun” guitar work on Loser, China Cat > I Know You Rider and Sugar Magnolia shines, while Brent’s keyboards add extra creativity; Phil’s bass drives the China‑Rider jam and Bob’s vocal call‑and‑response energizes the crowd. Overall the set flows superbly with standout moments that make it one of the most memorable early‑80s shows.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently enthusiastic 5‑star reviews praise both the musical performance and the crystal‑clear matrix recording; weighting gives a near‑top rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear sound with recent 16/24‑bit upgrades and re‑synced tracks; audience ambience retained without muddiness.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
I Can't Get No (Rolling Stones cover)
show_quality.setlist_flow: Energetic and well‑paced, with a powerful first half and an epic second set that builds momentum through extended jams and crowd‑engaging sing‑alongs.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar solos are described as ‘machine‑gun’ quality with full tone and an edgy bite; a notable break in Sugar Magnolia is highlighted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass “bombs away” on the China‑Rider jam, providing a driving foundation throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob (Bobby) leads crowd participation on a Rolling Stones cover and Satisfaction, adding vocal excitement.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard creativity is praised as a highlight of the performance.
